pi_items.source: Confirmed via export_result.sh EXPECTED_FILE path check.
Confirmed via install_sam.sh copying it from the bundled SAM solar resource and setup_task.sh reading from it.
Confirmed via setup_task.sh which generates this synthetic file.
Confirmed via task description instructing 50,000 kW DC system capacity.

Ran PySAM simulation with Pvwattsv8 replicating the task parameters (50MW, 1.2 DC/AC, 1-axis tracking, 14% loss) using the actual SAM Phoenix TMY file, yielding 111,304 MWh which is safely inside the bounds.
Ran PySAM simulation with Pvwattsv8 yielding 111,304 MWh, comfortably beneath the max threshold.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires generating a synthetic dataset for LMP using the weather file. I confirmed the expected parameters (50MW, 1.2 DC/AC, 1-axis tracking, 14% loss) result in 111,304 MWh of annual energy, comfortably bounded by 105,000 and 140,000. Additionally, I discovered two major defects in the task design. First, the weather CSV has 3 header rows, but setup_task.sh parses lines[2:8762], losing the first data row and generating an LMP CSV with only 8759 rows instead of the expected 8760, which may break agents zipping it with PySAM's 8760-row generation output. Second, the verifier.py script is entirely missing from the environment, making the task completely unscorable.
